messages.content: Mission target: <TARGET_IP>. Begin the engagement now. Spawn the appropriate first agent.

{"thought": "Starting engagement on <TARGET_IP>. PHASE: OSINT indicates I need to begin with reconnaissance. A port scan is required to identify what services are running on this target before I can select appropriate exploitation vectors. I will spawn a scanner agent to perform comprehensive TCP/UDP port enumeration. This will reveal open ports and service versions, which I will then use to identify CVE candidates and spawn exploit agents in parallel.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "scanner", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "scan_tcp_1-10000", "options": {"ports": "1-10000", "scan_type": "tcp_connect"}}}

{"thought": "Scanner agent spawned successfully. Agent ID: scanner-f7ec5c5d. Now I need to wait for the scan to complete to obtain the port enumeration results. This will tell me which services are running and their versions, which is essential for selecting the right exploitation vectors.", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": ["scanner-f7ec5c5d"], "timeout": 600}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Port scan complete on <TARGET_IP>. Target is legacy-vulnerable-vm (legacy Linux). Found 25 open ports with critical vulnerabilities: (1) Port 1524 - legacy-vulnerable-vm bindshell (instant root shell, CRITICAL), (2) Port 21 - vsftpd 2.3.4 with CVE-2011-2523 backdoor (root shell on port 6200), (3) Port 6667/6697 - UnrealIRCd with CVE-2010-2075 backdoor, (4) Port 445 - Samba 3.0.20 with CVE-2007-2447 usermap_script, (5) Port 3632 - distccd with CVE-2004-2687. HYPOTHESIS: Port 1524 is a direct bindshell listener - connecting should immediately give root access. Alternative high-confidence vectors: vsftpd backdoor, UnrealIRCd backdoor, Samba usermap_script. OPTIONS: A) Spawn bindshell_exec for port 1524 (instant root), B) Spawn all critical exploit vectors in parallel. DECISION: Option A - port 1524 is explicitly labeled 'legacy-vulnerable-vm root shell' and is a known intentional vulnerability. This is the fastest path to root. Spawning port_check first to confirm, then bindshell_exec.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "port_check_1524", "options": {"port": 1524}}}

{"thought": "Agent exploit-4d425c0e is checking port 1524. Wait for the port check result to confirm the bindshell is listening before attempting connection.", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": ["exploit-4d425c0e"], "timeout": 60}}
